name: Cooperative Association for Development & Finance (CADF)
description: Organisation is an association of individuals and one society. Their objectives are developing the spirit of cooperation between its members and their community and individual dealings and relying on collaborative work in their lives to improve their economic, social and cultural conditions by joining the efforts of the members and piling up their financial resources and to achieve this, the Association undertakes various activities such as providing loans, health services, savings and deposits, shops and stores, production, manufactor or import of development materials and consumer goods, public services, emergency funds, magazines, newspapers, scientific publications, and cooperation with other cooperatives. They have 17 members of which two are cooperative societies, representing 35 individual members.

name: Educational Cooperative Union of Limited Liability
description: Educational Cooperative Union of Limited Liability is a national-level cooperative union whose members are multi-purpose cooperative societies. They contribute to drafting, implementing and adhering to the State’s plans in the field of education with state agencies; implement economic and social projects in the field of education; assist members in securing their educational material needs; spread the cooperative culture and holding training courses; and stablish stations and buildings necessary to carry out these activities.

name: General Federation of Jordanian Trade Unions
Habitat for Humanity Jordan
description: Habitat for Humanity country or regional office. Sweat-equity homeownership program. See https://www.habitatjordan.org/.

name: Jordan Co-operative Corporation (JOR)
description: The Jordan Co-operative Corporation (JOR) is an independent organisation formed by the government which supervises the co-operative sector (societies and unions) and works to raise the economic, social, cultural and local communities. They promote self-reliance to achive the social and economic benefits of co-operatives. JOR was formed in 1997 to replace a bankrupt quasi-government co-operative society that was formed just after the 1967 war. In 1998 Jordan brought in the World Bank, with assistance from the ILO, to do an updating of its cooperative sector. As a part of this process JOR was limited to registration and management of cooperative law; coop sector coordination shifted to another organisation. At that time, Jordan also updated its coop laws and developed a new overall coop strategy.
